subject: Cloud Computing
title: The GPU Cloud Wars
description: A 60-second lesson on The GPU Cloud Wars.

hook.text: NVIDIA H100 GPUs cost $30,000 each and sell out instantly. Cloud providers are in an arms race to stockpile them.

buildup.text: Every major AI company needs GPU clusters. Providers, startups, and governments all compete for limited supply.

discovery.text: New GPU cloud startups (CoreWeave, Lambda Labs) offer pure GPU compute without the complexity of traditional cloud.

twist.text: Custom AI chips are emerging: Google TPUs, AWS Trainium, and Microsoft's Maia. The GPU monopoly may not last forever.

climax.text: The GPU shortage is reshaping cloud economics. AI compute is the new premium resource, like storage was in the 2000s.

punchline.text: GPUs are the new gold. Cloud providers fight for every chip.

quiz.question: How are cloud providers responding to the GPU shortage?
options: By developing custom AI chips to reduce dependence on NVIDIA GPUs
By stopping all AI services until more GPUs are available
By sharing their GPU inventory with competing cloud providers
